title: rubus patuliformis
intro: Rubus patuliformis, also known as creeping raspberry or spreading raspberry, is a type of bramble plant native to China, characterized by its thorny stems and edible fruits. Traditionally, Rubus patuliformis has been used in Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antibacterial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as lowering blood pressure, reducing cholesterol levels, and aiding in digestion, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. Rubus patuliformis can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system.
